threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'just popping to the shop' means a quick trip. It actually involves a 20-minute detour because of a pop-up market.
Saw a guy trying to pay with a banana instead of coins last Friday on the 35. The driver didn’t even blink. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in a tuxedo chatting with a group of kids by the playground, while a lady in a headscarf sold homemade baklava next to him. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a cocktail bar that serves drinks in jars while an old man plays polka on an accordion. It’s weird, but it works.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's a local if they can name all the pupusas spots and still argue about Mission tacos without breaking a sweat.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a whiff of street tacos while waiting for the 21 at 7th and Market, saw a Muni driver giving a thumbs up to a guy juggling. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that the best bars in Gràcia don't have menus; just ask the locals about the day’s specials. It's like a secret handshake.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still ask for 'tapas' in a sit-down restaurant, instead of just diving into the 'pintxos' at the bar.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of just the Overground like everyone else. It's a dead giveaway, mate.
Caught the N133 last week; a bloke was arguing with a pigeon over a chip. Only in Brixton would a seagull join in for backup.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I found a cat lounging on the roof of my balcony, soaking up the morning sun while I had my coffee. That's when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble upon a rooftop cinema screening classic films with a view of the TV tower—makes for a magical summer night.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission if they’ve had a necessary detour due to the dog that’s always lounging on the tracks near 16th St. Muni.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy calmly sweep a pile of pigeon feathers off the Muni steps before boarding. Only in San Francisco do we have genteel bird custodians.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the locals know it’s the tiny bars on Carrer de l'Or that hide the real character of the place.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the evening noise; they’re still adjusting to the locals’ late-night revelry in Plaça del Sol.
